rationale: Okada (2005) is cited in the conceptual development section, not just in a peripheral literature list. The citation supports a key premise used to motivate hypotheses: consumers need justification to indulge. The paper explicitly states that it builds on this notion, making the cited work a conceptual foundation for the new deservingness mechanism, even though no direct method or measure is reused.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Okada (2005) as part of the core justification-for-indulgence literature, arguing that indulgent choices require justification and decline when justification is weak. It then develops its own account by introducing deservingness as an additional justification source triggered by relationship reminders.
